title: MetaLend C-02: Fake Oracle Price Manipulation
summary: A deep dive into how a missing access control check allowed attackers to update the oracle price in the MetaLend protocol.

`,(0,s.jsxDEV)(t.p,{children:"The protocol uses a custom oracle contract to fetch asset prices. The contract was designed to be updated periodically by a trusted off-chain bot. However, the update function was completely public:"},void 0,!1,{fileName:"/Users/ramprasadgoud/Downloads/building/codekashi/content/posts/_mdx_bundler_entry_point-054af6b2-5298-4b75-b447-eddf2284955c.mdx",lineNumber:17,columnNumber:1},this),`
`,(0,s.jsxDEV)(t.pre,{children:(0,s.jsxDEV)(t.code,{className:"language-solidity",children:`function updatePrice(address asset, uint256 newPrice) public {
    require(asset != address(0), "Invalid asset");
    require(newPrice > 0, "Invalid price");
    
    // @audit-issue Missing access control! Any user can call this.
    prices[asset] = newPrice;
    lastUpdateTime[asset] = block.timestamp;
    
    emit PriceUpdated(asset, newPrice);
}

`,(0,s.jsxDEV)(t.p,{children:["The fix is straightforward: implement proper access control. + function updatePrice(address asset, uint256 newPrice) public onlyOracle {
      require(asset != address(0), "Invalid asset");
      require(newPrice > 0, "Invalid price");
      
      prices[asset] = newPrice;
      lastUpdateTime[asset] = block.timestamp;
      
      emit PriceUpdated(asset, newPrice);
